title: The Amber Signal
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A late-night radio engineer hears a coded distress call in a dead frequency.
long_description:
The Amber Signal follows Mara Sable, an adult late-night radio engineer, through a postwar city of rooftops and radio towers as a late-night radio engineer hears a coded distress call in a dead frequency. The inquiry moves through offices, sealed rooms, public hearings, and physical evidence, keeping its suspense grounded in work, speech, and consequence.

As Len Orta, Ruth Bell, and Inspector Dario Vale help test the record, Otto Hain tries to turn delay into truth. The novel is clean adult suspense about institutions, memory, and the cost of naming what everyone was paid not to see.
